subject: Ecology
title: Salinity and Marine Life
description: A 60-second lesson on Salinity and Marine Life.

hook.text: Seawater is about 3.5% salt—roughly a teaspoon per cup. Life in the ocean is built around it.

buildup.text: Marine organisms evolved to balance salt and water across their cell membranes constantly.

discovery.text: Estuaries, where rivers meet the sea, have shifting salinity that only specialists survive.

twist.text: Salmon switch between salt and freshwater systems—a rare feat that reshapes river ecosystems.

climax.text: Melting glaciers dilute coastal salinity, stressing species adapted to stable salt levels.

punchline.text: Salt shapes who can live where in the sea.
